query: I was flying and the engine just got weaker and weaker. Like it was losing its strength.
fsm_probable_cause: Carburetor icing / Plugged fuel strainer / Defective magneto
fsm_remedy: Apply carburetor heat / Clean strainer / Replace magneto
gus_diagnosis: The engine losing strength progressively during flight indicates a reduction in power output. We need to narrow down whether this is a fuel delivery issue, an ignition problem, an air intake restriction, or something more severe. To start, observe the engine's response to controls and check for basic system functionality.
gus_reasoning: The progressive loss of engine strength points to a fundamental issue affecting power production. The most probable causes involve the fuel, ignition, or induction systems, as these directly control combustion efficiency. A restriction in the fuel system (lines, vent, carburetor) would progressively starve the engine, leading to weakening. Similarly, degrading ignition components (spark plugs, harness, magnetos) or a restricted air filter would reduce combustion efficiency and power output. Less common but still plausible are internal engine mechanical issues (low compression) or a restricted exhaust system. I will start by asking about observable symptoms related to fuel delivery, ignition performance, and air intake, as these are the most common and often easiest to verify without extensive disassembly.
gus_answer_paths: When you slowly pull the mixture control to idle cut-off, does the RPM rise slightly before dropping, or does it drop immediately and sharply?
During a magneto check (if performed), was there an excessive RPM drop on either magneto, or a rough running condition?
Was there any indication of carburetor icing, and did applying carburetor heat have any effect on engine power or smoothness?
Were there any unusual engine noises, vibrations, or abnormal gauge readings (oil pressure, oil temperature, cylinder head temperature) when the power loss began?
